title: Browser Origins, CORS, CSRF, Cookies, and Security Fields · Runtime Contract: Csrf Crossoriginprotection
summary: A browser API reflects arbitrary Origin with credentials, mutates state on GET, accepts ambient cookies cross-site, caches preflight incorrectly, and treats CORS as server authorization. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo csrf crossoriginprotection increment in browser request trust and cross-origin policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use safe-method invariants, Go CrossOriginProtection, Sec-Fetch-Site and Origin policy, tokens when needed, and narrowly reviewed bypasses.
Validate exact origins, methods, requested headers, credential mode, exposed headers, max age, Vary, null origin, and rejection behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
